category_subtitle: Learn how to count letters, messages, or mail in Japanese using 通
category_detail:
Counting Letters in Japanese

In Japanese, letters, mail, documents, notices, and messages are commonly counted with the counter 「通」.

This counter is often used for things like letters, emails, messages, postcards, formal notices, and official documents.

Important Reading Pattern
The counter 通 has some sound changes, so learners should pay attention to pronunciation.

Examples:
1通 (いっつう / ittsuu)
3通 (さんつう / santsuu)
6通 (ろくつう / rokutsuu)
8通 (はっつう / hattsuu)
10通 (じゅっつう / juttsuu)

Common Usage
手紙を一通送りました。 (tegami o ittsuu okurimashita)
Meaning: I sent one letter.

メールを二通受け取りました。 (meeru o nitsuu uketorimashita)
Meaning: I received two emails.

Important Notes
This counter is useful not only for physical letters, but also for written communication such as emails and messages in many contexts.

This category focuses on the most useful and natural learner-friendly readings for counting letters and mail.

item_list.description: This uses a sound change and must be memorized.
A regular reading used for two letters, emails, or documents.
よんつう is the standard learner-friendly reading. しつう may also be heard.

item_list.hiragana: ななつう / しちつう
item_list.description: ななつう is very common and easier for learners. しちつう is also possible.

item_list.hiragana: はっつう / はちつう
item_list.description: はっつう is the standard reading. はちつう may appear in careful speech, but learners should remember はっつう.
きゅうつう is the standard reading.

item_list.hiragana: じゅっつう / じっつう
item_list.description: じゅっつう is very common. じっつう is also heard. Both are acceptable.
This follows the same sound pattern as 1通, so it becomes いっつう.

item_list.hiragana: じゅうよんつう / じゅうしつう
item_list.description: じゅうよんつう is more common and recommended for learners. じゅうしつう may also be heard.

item_list.hiragana: じゅうななつう / じゅうしちつう
item_list.description: じゅうななつう is very common and easier for learners. じゅうしちつう is also possible.

item_list.hiragana: じゅうはっつう / じゅうはちつう
item_list.description: じゅうはっつう is the standard reading. じゅうはちつう may appear in careful speech, but learners should remember じゅうはっつう.

item_list.hiragana: にじゅっつう / にじっつう
item_list.description: にじゅっつう is very common. にじっつう is also heard. Both are acceptable.
